mrmopt 0.1.0
  • Articles

  • Roadmap

    Simulated multi-channel media spend dataset

    mrmopt_data.Rd

    A simulated dataset containing weekly media spend and conversions for five advertising channels over two years (104 weeks). The data are generated from known response curves (Gompertz, Logistic, and Log-Logistic) with additive Gaussian noise, and are intended for use in package examples and vignettes.

    Usage

    mrmopt_data

    Format

    A tibble with 520 rows and 4 columns:

    channel

    Factor. Advertising channel name. One of "Paid Search", "Paid Social", "Display", "Online Video", "TV".

    week

    Date. Week start date (Monday), ranging from 2023-01-02 to 2024-12-23.

    spend

    Integer. Weekly media spend in US dollars.

    conversions

    Integer. Weekly conversions (KPI).

    Details

    Each channel has a distinct spend scale and response curve shape:

    • Paid Search: Gompertz curve, ~$45k/week average spend.

    • Paid Social: Logistic curve, ~$27k/week average spend.

    • Display: Gompertz curve, ~$18k/week average spend.

    • Online Video: Logistic curve, ~$34k/week average spend.

    • TV: Log-Logistic curve, ~$80k/week average spend.

    The dataset is generated by data-raw/mrmopt_data.R.

    Examples

    data(mrmopt_data)
mrmopt_data
#> # A tibble: 520 × 4
#>    channel     week       spend conversions
#>    <fct>       <date>     <dbl>       <int>
#>  1 Paid Search 2023-01-02 46306         616
#>  2 Paid Search 2023-01-09 38396         171
#>  3 Paid Search 2023-01-16 41434         334
#>  4 Paid Search 2023-01-23 46644         687
#>  5 Paid Search 2023-01-30 37157         166
#>  6 Paid Search 2023-02-06 47482         716
#>  7 Paid Search 2023-02-13 32331          45
#>  8 Paid Search 2023-02-20 28702          83
#>  9 Paid Search 2023-02-27 47682         739
#> 10 Paid Search 2023-03-06 48444         743
#> # ℹ 510 more rows

# Filter to a single channel
paid_search <- mrmopt_data[mrmopt_data$channel == "Paid Search", ]
